requirement_text: Joined federates shall use Declaration Management services to declare intent to generate information.

requirement_text: The RTI shall use DM, DDM, Object Management, Ownership Management, and the FDD hierarchies to determine allowed object registration classes.

requirement_text: The RTI shall use DM, DDM, Object Management, Ownership Management, and the FDD hierarchies to determine object discovery classes.

requirement_text: The RTI shall use DM, DDM, Object Management, Ownership Management, and the FDD hierarchies to determine interactions available for sending.

requirement_text: The effects of Declaration Management services shall be independent of federate logical time.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.

requirement_text: Every class shall have at most one immediate superclass.

requirement_text: Class hierarchy validation shall reject inheritance cycles.

requirement_text: Every object class shall have an associated set of class attributes declared in the FDD.

requirement_text: The implementation shall identify inherited object class attributes from superclasses.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.1.

requirement_text: The implementation shall compute available attributes as declared attributes plus inherited attributes.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.1.

requirement_text: Every interaction class shall have an associated set of parameters declared in the FDD.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.1.

requirement_text: The implementation shall compute available parameters as declared parameters plus inherited parameters.

requirement_text: Services taking an object class and attribute set shall accept only available attributes of that object class.

requirement_text: A class attribute shall be usable in publish, unpublish, subscribe, and unsubscribe object-class-attribute services only when it is available for the specified object class.

requirement_text: The RTI shall maintain subscribed attributes per joined federate and object class from subscribe/unsubscribe service history.

requirement_text: Subscribe and unsubscribe actions for one object class shall not alter subscriptions for another object class.

requirement_text: A subscribed class attribute shall be either actively subscribed or passively subscribed for a joined federate at an object class, but not both.

requirement_text: The RTI shall maintain explicitly published attributes per joined federate and object class from publish/unpublish service history.

requirement_text: The first non-empty publication for an object class shall establish publication of that object class at the joined federate.

requirement_text: The RTI shall support implicit publication of HLAprivilegeToDeleteObject when at least one non-privilege attribute of the class is explicitly published.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: The RTI shall not implicitly publish HLAprivilegeToDeleteObject if that attribute has been explicitly published since publication was most recently established.

requirement_text: Publish and unpublish actions for one object class shall not alter publications for another object class.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: An object class shall be considered published for a joined federate iff at least one attribute of the class is published.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: Joined federates shall register object instances only for object classes they have published.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: The registered class of an object instance shall be the object class supplied when that instance is registered.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: Discovery of an object instance shall establish a discovered class at the discovering joined federate.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: An object instance shall have at most one discovered class per joined federate while known.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: A known object's discovered class shall not change unless the federate locally deletes it and later discovers it again.

requirement_text: The known class at a discovering federate shall be the object's discovered class.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: The corresponding class attribute of an instance attribute shall be determined from the object's known class at the joined federate and the attribute designator.
source_note: Detailed Clause 5 extraction v0.4 from IEEE 1516.1-2010 Clause 5.1.2.

requirement_text: The RTI shall identify corresponding instance attributes for a class attribute across known unowned attributes and owned attributes at the joined federate.
